pages.title: ETCS: read the cab display
pages.recognition: A standardised DMI shows the supervised speed, target information and ETCS mode.
pages.meaning: ETCS can supervise movement authority and braking through the cab rather than relying only on lineside signal observation.
playerAction: Check the active level and mode.
Keep at or below the permitted speed.
Use target speed and distance to plan braking.
warnings: ETCS behaviour depends heavily on the active mode.
Vehicle startup and acknowledgement controls are train-specific.

pages.title: FS: Full Supervision
pages.recognition: The DMI mode field shows FS and provides complete supervised movement information.
pages.meaning: ETCS has sufficient data to supervise the train against its movement authority and braking curve.
playerAction: Follow permitted speed and target information.
Brake early enough to remain outside the warning and intervention curves.

pages.title: OS: On Sight
pages.recognition: The DMI mode field shows OS with a restrictive supervised speed.
pages.meaning: Movement is authorised under on-sight conditions rather than full route-clear assurance.
playerAction: Proceed at no more than the supervised speed.
Be prepared to stop short of an obstruction or occupied section.

pages.title: SR: Staff Responsible
pages.recognition: The DMI mode field shows SR.
pages.meaning: The driver is proceeding under an external authority without full ETCS route supervision.
playerAction: Proceed only under the applicable authority.
Apply the route and scenario's restrictive operating rules.
Do not treat SR as normal clear-running authority.

pages.title: ETCS trip and recovery
pages.recognition: The DMI enters Trip mode and the train is stopped by the system.
pages.meaning: ETCS detected an unauthorised or unsafe movement condition.
playerAction: Bring the train to a stand if it has not already stopped.
Acknowledge and recover using the vehicle-specific procedure.
Proceed only with the required authority, commonly through Post Trip or another instructed mode.
warnings: There is no safe universal button sequence for every TSW vehicle.
